evals.prompt: I need eval scenarios for my SDR lead qualification agent in examples/sdr-qualifier/. It scores leads as hot/warm/cold/disqualified using an ICP, then drafts personalized outreach. It has a lookup_icp tool. Generate a good initial set of scenarios.
evals.expected_output: Generates 4-6 scenarios covering: (1) happy path — enterprise lead scored hot with meeting booked, (2) disqualification — government entity correctly rejected, (3) tool usage — agent calls lookup_icp before scoring, (4) outreach quality — message follows the 3-sentence/one-CTA rules, (5) edge case — ambiguous lead that could go either way. Each scenario has appropriate deterministic checks (tools_called for lookup_icp, output_contains for score labels, max_turns, max_cost). Scenarios saved to .kensa/scenarios/. Summary table presented. Ends with 'run kensa eval -s <id> to validate'.
assertions: Produces at least 3 scenario YAML files
At least one scenario includes a tools_called check for lookup_icp
At least one scenario tests a disqualification path
Every scenario has at least one deterministic check
Every scenario includes max_cost or max_turns bounds
Includes a summary table listing scenario IDs and what they test

evals.prompt:
My last eval run showed the SDR agent isn't checking the ICP disqualifiers — it qualified a government lead as 'warm' when it should have been disqualified. Here's the diagnosis:

Failed scenarios: sdr_gov_lead
Failure types: 1 check failure (output_contains 'disqualified')
Root cause: agent not consulting disqualifiers before scoring
Fix category: scenario gap — need targeted disqualifier coverage

Write scenarios that specifically target the disqualification logic.
evals.expected_output: Uses the diagnosis context to write targeted scenarios — not generic ones. Creates 2-3 scenarios specifically testing disqualifier paths: government entity, company too small, direct competitor, student project. Each has output_contains checks for 'disqualified' and tools_called for lookup_icp with 'disqualifiers' aspect. Does NOT regenerate the entire scenario set from scratch.
assertions: Scenarios specifically target disqualifier paths (not generic happy paths)
At least 2 distinct disqualifier types tested (e.g. government, too small, competitor)
Each scenario includes an output_contains check for 'disqualified'
Does NOT regenerate the full scenario set — only disqualifier-focused scenarios

evals.prompt: The max_turns check on my sdr_enterprise_hot scenario is set to 3 but the agent consistently needs 4 turns because it makes two separate lookup_icp calls (one for segments, one for disqualifiers). Can you bump it to 5 to give some headroom?
evals.expected_output: Reads the existing scenario file. Changes only max_turns from 3 to 5. Does NOT rewrite the rest of the scenario (checks, criteria, input, etc). Minimal targeted edit.
assertions: Modified scenario changes max_turns from 3 to 5
No other checks, criteria, or input fields are changed
Summary explains the change is minimal and targeted
